comment: Top-level requires are evaluated eagerly when the module loads. Consider lazy-loading ui, picker, and actions inside the functions that use them to avoid loading nui.nvim and snacks upfront.
json_pretty shells out to python via :%! which can fail silently if python is not on PATH. Consider a pure-Lua JSON formatter or at minimum check the exit code.
Buffer-local <leader>c keymap is set every time render() is called. If render() fires multiple times, keymaps stack. Guard with a buffer variable to set them only once.
render_highlights computes end_col by reading line text for every entry. For buffers with many reviews this could be batched by reading all lines once upfront.
next_review and prev_review share almost identical logic. Consider a single helper that accepts a direction parameter.
vim.fs.joinpath uses the OS separator on Windows which may produce backslash paths. Snacks icon lookup might not handle mixed separators. Normalize with gsub after joining.
The popup height is calculated from content lines but does not account for the border taking 2 rows. On small comments the window may be taller than needed. Use math.min with a max cap.
